channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Kaduna APC members endorse President Tinubu for 2027 re-election bid
item.description: On Saturday, members of the All Progressives Congress (APC) across Kaduna State carried out a statewide affirmation exercise in which they unanimously endorsed President Bola Ahmed Tinubu as the party...

item.title: Royal Navy prepares potential mine-clearing mission in Strait of Hormuz amid regional tensions
item.description: The United Kingdom’s Royal Navy is preparing for a possible international mine-clearing operation in the Strait of Hormuz as tensions remain high following the conflict involving the United States, Is...

item.title: Economist Joachim Klement Predicts the Netherlands to Win the 2026 FIFA World Cup
item.description: German economist Joachim Klement, who has successfully predicted the winners of the last three FIFA World Cups, believes the Netherlands will win the 2026 tournament. Klement first created his forecas...

item.title: A Sydney woman found love with a restaurant owner after a chance meeting at a friend’s birthday event
item.description: Monika Ruggerino recounts how an unexpected series of events at a Sydney restaurant led to meeting her future husband, Antonio. In early 2015, while helping organise a friend’s 30th birthday at Verde ...

item.title: Kenya’s fuel supply stability amid regional shortages and the push towards electric vehicles
item.description: The article examines the current fuel situation in East Africa, highlighting how different countries are experiencing varying degrees of strain. Ethiopia, Uganda, and Tanzania are grappling with recur...

item.title: Emily Blunt discusses ongoing experience with stuttering
item.description: Actress Emily Blunt has openly shared her ongoing experience with stuttering, highlighting that it remains a condition she continues to manage in her daily life. In her recent remarks, she reflected o...

item.title: Addressing mental health challenges and harmful gender norms among boys in South Africa
item.description: The article highlights growing concern around the often-overlooked mental health struggles faced by young boys in South Africa, describing it as a “silent crisis” that requires urgent national attenti...

item.title: Pakistan-mediated talks show progress toward potential US-Iran understanding after high-level Tehran visit
item.description: According to the report, Pakistan’s Chief of Defence Forces, Field Marshal Asim Munir, concluded a high-level two-day visit to Tehran amid ongoing diplomatic efforts aimed at easing tensions between t...

item.title: Fuel prices continue to rise as oil companies face heavy under-recoveries amid global crude volatility
item.description: State-run oil marketing companies have increased petrol and diesel prices for the third time within 10 days, raising rates by nearly ₹1 per litre on May 24, 2026. Since May 15, petrol and diesel price...

item.title: Nikolaj Ehlers lifts Hurricanes to overtime win against Canadiens to level playoff series
item.description: The Carolina Hurricanes defeated the Montreal Canadiens in overtime after Nikolaj Ehlers scored the game-winning goal, allowing Carolina to even the playoff series. The matchup was closely contested t...

item.title: Humorous and Creative Vintage Novelty Photographs from the Early 20th Century
item.description: The article explores the playful and imaginative side of early photography through the lens of vintage novelty images. Contrary to the common belief that historical portraits were always serious and r...

item.title: Florida wildlife agency settles First Amendment lawsuit with fired biologist for $485,000
item.description: Former Florida Fish and Wildlife Conservation Commission biologist Brittney Brown reached a $485,000 settlement with the state of Florida after filing a lawsuit alleging that her First Amendment right...

item.title: Concerns over union strategy and potential disruption in UK schools amid planned education strikes
item.description: The article reports on tensions surrounding planned industrial action by the National Education Union (NEU) in the United Kingdom, which could see large-scale strikes involving hundreds of thousands o...

item.title: A critique of Pia Cayetano’s selective use of gender politics in Philippine Senate discourse
item.description: The article examines Senator Pia Cayetano’s recent emotional reaction in the Philippine Senate and situates it within what the author describes as a pattern of selective engagement with gender and wom...

item.title: Rising Consumer Insolvencies in Canada Push More People to Seek Debt Relief Support
item.description: Canada is experiencing a significant rise in consumer debt problems, with more than 37,000 people filing for insolvency during the first three months of 2026, the highest quarterly total since the aft...

item.title: Tinio criticises defence chief over claim linking all Toboso deaths to NPA rebels
item.description: Opposition figure Antonio Tinio has strongly criticised Defence Secretary Gilbert Teodoro Jr. following his statement that all fatalities in Toboso were members of the New People’s Army (NPA). In a re...

item.title: India fertility rate falls to 1.9; only six states remain above replacement level
item.description: India’s fertility pattern continues to show a steady decline, according to the latest Sample Registration System (SRS) report for 2024. The country’s total fertility rate (TFR) has dropped to 1.9 from...

item.title: Immigration Service reassures public over border security amid Ebola concerns
item.description: The Nigeria Immigration Service (NIS) has reassured Nigerians that the country’s land borders are properly secured and not porous, despite growing concerns over the spread of Ebola in parts of Central...

item.title: María Félix: Life and Legacy of a Golden Age Mexican Film Icon
item.description: María Félix (1914–2002) was one of the most influential figures in the Golden Age of Mexican cinema, widely admired for her commanding screen presence, striking beauty, and strong-willed personality. ...

item.title: Canadian activists detained during Gaza aid flotilla return home after deportation from Israel
item.description: Nine of the 12 Canadians who participated in a flotilla attempting to deliver symbolic humanitarian aid to Gaza are expected to return to Canada this weekend after being detained by Israeli forces. Th...
